channel.title: The Financial Express – Economy & Trade
channel.description: Latest Economy and Trade news from The Financial Express Bangladesh

item.title: Gold falls from three-week high on profit-booking, firm dollar
item.description: Gold prices fell on Tuesday as investors booked profits after bullion rose more than 2% in the previous session, while pressure from a stronger dollar also weighed on the yellow metal. item.title: BB warns of remittance risks ahead amid ME tensions
item.description: The central bank has cautioned that inward remittance flows could come under pressure as geopolitical tensions in the Middle East disrupt labour migration and weaken economic activity in major host countries. item.title: April private credit growth 4.75pc
item.description: Formal credit growth in the private sector remains almost static, reaching 4.75 per cent in April, signalling a deep slowdown in the country's business activities. The low trend in credit demand from private enterprises is attributed to banks becoming more cautious amid rising non-performing loans

item.title: Remedy for energy crisis: JS special body places 12-point must-dos
item.description: Augmenting strategic fuel reserves to cover at least three months' demand, diversifying import sources and introducing full digital monitoring of the energy-supply system are among the must-dos recommended by a special parliamentary committee for strengthening Bangladesh's long-term energy security

item.description:

item.description: A parliamentary special committee has proposed expanding strategic fuel reserves to cover a minimum of three months of demand, while urging diversified import sources and stronger measures to curb hoarding and smuggling. item.title: ECNEC to review 13 development projects at Tuesday’s meeting
item.description: The Executive Committee of the National Economic Council (ECNEC) is set to consider 13 development projects at its 12th meeting of the fiscal year 2025-26, scheduled to be held on Tuesday at the Bangladesh Secretariat. item.title: Inflation climbs to 9.42pc in May amid higher food prices
item.description: Bangladesh’s point-to-point inflation rose to 9.42 per cent in May 2026, driven mainly by an increase in food prices, according to the latest data released by the Bangladesh Bureau of Statistics (BBS). item.description: The National Board of Revenue (NBR) has extended the deadline for businesses to enter previously submitted paper-based VAT returns into the electronic VAT (e-VAT) system until June 30, 2026, ahead of the planned introduction of mandatory online VAT return filing from July. item.title: RSF seeks end to BRTC bus-leasing practice
item.description: The Road Safety Foundation (RSF) has called for an immediate end to the practice of leasing Bangladesh Road Transport Corporation (BRTC) buses to educational institutions and other public and private organisations, arguing that it deprives ordinary commuters of affordable public transport services.

item.description: Bangladesh Start-up Investment Company (BSIC) Tuesday launched its maiden fund titled 'Onkur1' with Finance and Planning Minister Amir Khosru Mahmud Chowdhury pledging political non-interference in the operations of the country's financial sector. item.title: Industrial term lending jumps 26pc in Q2
item.description: Disbursement of industrial term loans rose by over 26 per cent to Tk 312.47 billion during the October-December period of fiscal year FY 2025-26 over that of the previous quarter. item.title: India raises gold and silver tariffs to 15pc to curb imports
item.description: India has raised import tariffs on gold and silver to 15 per cent from 6 per cent, government orders ​said on Wednesday, as part of efforts to curb overseas purchases of the ‌metals and ease pressure on the country's foreign exchange reserves. item.title: Iraq, Pakistan strike energy deals with Iran
item.description: Both Iraq and Pakistan have cut deals with Iran to ship oil and liquefied natural gas from the Gulf, according to five sources with knowledge of the matter, in a demonstration of ​Tehran's ability to control energy flows through the Strait of Hormuz. item.title: MCCI sees brighter economic outlook despite inflation
item.description: The performances of the key economic indicators are expected to grow during the April-June period, although inflationary pressures are expected to persist. The Metropolitan Chamber of Commerce and Industry (MCCI), Dhaka, made the observations in its quarterly economic report unveiled on Tuesday.

item.description: The government is preparing a major expansion of social-safety-net spending in the next fiscal year (FY 2026-27), with Tk 170 billion allocation to implement three flagship election pledges of the ruling Bangladesh Nationalist Party (BNP). item.description: The Trading Corporation of Bangladesh (TCB) is grappling with mounting financial pressure as soaring procurement costs and high-interest bank loans have pushed its subsidy burden beyond Tk 6.15 billion in just three months, sources say. item.description: The Asian Development Bank (ADB) committed to providing a US$100 million soft loan for strengthening the Universal Pension System project, with a feasibility study currently underway, officials said. item.title: Govt to buy 20 million litres of palm oil for TCB sales
item.description: The government is buying 20 million litres of refined palm oil to sell at a lower price to low-income people holding Trading Corporation of Bangladesh (TCB) cards. item.description: The Trump administration said on Monday it will loan energy companies 53.3 million barrels of crude from the US Strategic Petroleum Reserve as part of a global agreement to calm oil markets that have spiked on the US-Israeli war with Iran. item.description: Bangladesh Bank has purchased dollars from commercial banks for two consecutive days in the second week of May.
